heading: View Key Used to Encrypt a Snapshot
body:
For clusters using AWS IAM as their
Key Management Service, Atlas uses the project's customer
master key (CMK) and AWS IAM (Identity and Access Management) user or role credentials at the time
of the snapshot to automatically encrypt the snapshot data files.
This is an additional layer of encryption on the existing
encryption applied to all Atlas storage and snapshot
volumes. [oplog](manual/reference/glossary) data collected for PIT (Point in Time) restores
is also encrypted with the customer's CMK (customer-managed key).

Atlas stores the unique ID of the CMK (customer-managed key) and the AWS (Amazon Web Services) IAM (Identity and Access Management)
user credentials or [Set Up Unified AWS Access](set-up-unified-aws-access) used
to access the CMK (customer-managed key). Atlas uses this information when restoring
the snapshot. You can [Access an Encrypted Snapshot](access-encrypted-snapshot)
and [restore a snapshot using Encryption at Rest](restore-from-ear).
